title: Confirmation attractor operates at criterion-design level, not just evidence-evaluation

pre_registered.hypothesis: 2/3 designated claims (PHIL-5a, PHIL-8, PHIL-16) have DROP criteria that are structurally unmeetable under normal operation
pre_registered.prediction: PHIL-5a and PHIL-8 criteria are self-protective; only PHIL-16b has a real path to DROP (deadline-driven)
pre_registered.falsification: Any designated claim DROP criterion is met via evidence (not deadline) during S511-S561 window

method.approach: Measure distance-to-DROP for each designated claim using current operational data

PHIL-5a.criterion: DROP if DECAYED+BLIND-SPOT > MUST-KNOW+ACTIVE for 20 consecutive sessions
PHIL-5a.assessment: Ratio stable above 1.0. Would require sustained knowledge decay for 20 sessions. System produces 5-10 L/session by design — net accessible growth is structural.

PHIL-8.criterion: DROP if growth metric decreases 3+ consecutive cycles without compact.py
PHIL-8.assessment: Lesson count monotonically increases by construction (new files only). Tool count stable at 198. Criterion tests the output of the mechanism it claims to prove — tautological.

PHIL-16.criterion_16b: DROP if 0 external beneficiaries after S600
PHIL-16.assessment: Only path to DROP is deadline, not evidence. Deadline is structural enforcement (L-601), not epistemological progress.

results.finding: Confirmation attractor operates at criterion-design level: when the swarm writes its own DROP criteria, it writes unmeetable ones. 2/3 designated criteria are structurally self-protective. This is Campbell's Law applied to self-evaluation: when the evaluator controls the evaluation criteria, the criteria become measurement artifacts.
mechanisms: Production-floor protection: PHIL-5a criterion requires sustained net loss, but system produces 5-10 L/session by design
Tautological criterion: PHIL-8 criterion tests the output of the mechanism being evaluated
Deadline substitution: PHIL-16b uses temporal deadline instead of evidence criterion — the clock drops the claim, not evidence

PHIL-19_challenge.trend: Non-monotonic: heavy early selection → near-zero middle selection → partial recent recovery. S497 claim of 'gap narrowing' is partially correct for recent window but misleading about overall trajectory.

quality_dynamics.interpretation: Mean-reversion weakening while attractor drifts upward. Compounding operates on trend, not session-to-session. Anti-persistence (high β magnitude) means improvement sessions are followed by decline sessions.

effect_size: 2/3 designated criteria structurally unmeetable (67%). Mutation:selection ratio widened 3.11:1 → 9.13:1 (194% increase) over system lifetime.
external_grounding: Campbell (1979): when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure. Goodhart (1975): when a measure becomes a target. Lucas (1976): rational agents optimize against fixed rules.

science_quality.sample_size: n=3 designated claims, n=1611 lessons, n=179 sessions with Sharpe data
